title: Shared Sources, Tenant Subscriptions, State Machines, and Races · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds idempotent shared-source subscription service from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of idempotent shared-source subscription service while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.
Create and remove tenant-scoped follows without deleting shared source history and expose explicit desired-state semantics.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appealed transitions with actors and causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Source Registration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 4391 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile shared sources, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle transitions, authorization, and registration races; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Source Registration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Source Registration, keep Feed Ts Source Registration connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Every tenant requires a separate publisher source and fetch schedule. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
